message:
feat(core,render,wasm): add CSS-only diagram animations with entrance, flow, pulse, and hover effects

Implement configurable CSS animation system for SVG diagrams (bd-3gq.4):
- Add animation_css() generator with entrance fade/slide, dashed-edge flow,
  highlighted-node pulse, and hover scale effects
- Emit --fm-enter-order CSS custom properties on nodes/edges for staggered
  sequencing based on topological rank
- Wire animation config (duration, stagger, flow speed, dash pattern,
  hover scale) through SvgRenderConfig and WASM SvgConfigOverrides
- Respect prefers-reduced-motion with blanket disable rule
- Animations are off by default; enabled via animations_enabled flag
- Promote font_size(), current_used_total_ms(), and apply() to const fn;
  change is_default_gantt_task_type to take GanttTaskType by value
- Close beads bd-3gq.3 (icons) and bd-3gq.4 (animations)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-3gq.1","title":"Implement advanced SVG visual effects: shadows, gradients, glow","description":"Add premium visual effects to SVG output that make diagrams look professional and polished:

1. DROP SHADOWS: Configurable SVG filter with feDropShadow. Soft shadow behind nodes and clusters. Adjustable: offset, blur radius, color, opacity. Default: subtle 2px offset, 4px blur, 20% opacity black.

2. GRADIENTS: Linear and radial gradient fills for nodes. Subtle top-to-bottom gradient gives depth. Configurable: gradient direction, start/end colors, midpoint.

3. GLOW EFFECT: Outer glow on selected/highlighted nodes. SVG filter with feGaussianBlur + feColorMatrix. Used for hover states and focus indicators.

4. ROUNDED EVERYTHING: Generous corner radii on all rectangular elements. Configurable globally and per-element. Default: 8px for nodes, 12px for clusters.

5. BORDER REFINEMENT: Thin, precise borders with antialiased rendering. Option for double borders on important nodes. Dashed borders for optional/external elements.

6. OPACITY LAYERS: Background clusters at 5-10% opacity for subtle grouping. Inactive/dimmed elements at 40% opacity for focus context.

All effects must be optional and configurable via theme. Performance: effects use SVG filters which are GPU-accelerated in browsers.

## Acceptance Criteria

- All functionality explicitly described above is implemented fully; no scope reduction, no feature deletions, and no silent behavioral regressions.
- Code is modular, documented where needed, and integrated with existing crates/interfaces without compatibility shims or duplicated logic.
- Comprehensive automated tests are added/updated for this issue: focused unit tests + relevant integration coverage for touched behavior and edge cases.
- End-to-end validation is covered where applicable (CLI and/or WASM path) with detailed structured logging (inputs, detection results, node/edge counts, output sizes, timings, and pass/fail reasons).
- Diagnostics are actionable: failures/warnings include precise context and suggested remediation.
- Determinism and stability expectations are verified for affected outputs (layout/render/serialization as applicable).
- Quality gates pass: `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo check --all-targets`, and relevant `cargo test` scope.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T16:36:19.570153381Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T06:13:50.604274100Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T06:13:50.604239816Z","close_reason":"Completed: configurable shadows/gradients/glow, border/opacity effect classes, and tests for fm-render-svg visual effects","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["polish","svg"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3gq.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1y5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3gq.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1y5.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3gq.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3gq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3gq.2","title":"Implement advanced typography and text rendering","description":"Elevate text rendering quality in SVG diagrams:

1. FONT STACK: Default to modern system font stack (Inter, -apple-system, Segoe UI, etc.). Monospace stack for code labels (JetBrains Mono, Fira Code, etc.).

2. TEXT HIERARCHY: Node labels use medium weight, edge labels use regular weight, cluster titles use semibold. Configurable size scale.

3. TEXT FITTING: Intelligent label sizing that shrinks font size to fit within node bounds while maintaining minimum readable size (10px). Prefer ellipsis over tiny text.

4. MULTI-LINE LABELS: Proper text wrapping with SVG tspan elements. Break on word boundaries. Respect explicit newline characters in labels.

5. MARKDOWN IN LABELS: Support basic markdown in labels: bold, italic, code, strikethrough. Render as styled tspan elements.

6. TEXT MEASUREMENT: Approximate text width using character-count heuristic with font-specific correction factors. Allow calibration via config. This drives accurate node sizing.

7. LETTER SPACING: Subtle letter-spacing increase (0.01em) for labels to improve readability at small sizes.

## Acceptance Criteria

- All functionality explicitly described above is implemented fully; no scope reduction, no feature deletions, and no silent behavioral regressions.
- Code is modular, documented where needed, and integrated with existing crates/interfaces without compatibility shims or duplicated logic.
- Comprehensive automated tests are added/updated for this issue: focused unit tests + relevant integration coverage for touched behavior and edge cases.
- End-to-end validation is covered where applicable (CLI and/or WASM path) with detailed structured logging (inputs, detection results, node/edge counts, output sizes, timings, and pass/fail reasons).
- Diagnostics are actionable: failures/warnings include precise context and suggested remediation.
- Determinism and stability expectations are verified for affected outputs (layout/render/serialization as applicable).
- Quality gates pass: `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo check --all-targets`, and relevant `cargo test` scope.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T16:36:19.646776755Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T03:01:32.708070151Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["polish","typography"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3gq.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1y5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3gq.2","depends_on_id":"bd-3gq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3gq.3","title":"Add icon/emoji support in diagram nodes","description":"Support icons and emojis in diagram nodes for visual context:

1. EMOJI SUPPORT: Render emoji characters natively in SVG text elements. Detect emoji codepoints and ensure proper sizing (emoji are typically wider than text).

2. ICON LIBRARY: Embed a curated set of common diagram icons as SVG path data: database cylinder, server rack, cloud, user silhouette, gear/settings, lock/security, API endpoint, mobile device, desktop, container/docker, queue, cache, load balancer, etc.

3. ICON SYNTAX: Support fa:icon-name (FontAwesome reference), ::icon(name) (mermaid syntax), and Unicode emoji in node labels.

4. ICON POSITIONING: Icon rendered above or to the left of label text within the node. Configurable position. Auto-size node to accommodate icon + label.

5. CUSTOM ICONS: Allow users to provide SVG icon definitions via config. Referenced by name in diagram syntax.

## Acceptance Criteria

-{"id":"bd-3gq.4","title":"Add CSS animation support for SVG diagrams","description":"Add optional CSS animations to SVG diagrams for enhanced visual communication:

1. ENTRANCE ANIMATIONS: Nodes and edges fade/slide in sequentially based on topological order. Creates a build-up effect showing the diagram structure progressively.

2. FLOW ANIMATIONS: Dashed edges with animated dash-offset to show data/control flow direction. Speed and dash pattern configurable.

3. PULSE ANIMATION: Gentle pulse on highlighted/active nodes. Used for drawing attention to specific elements.

4. HOVER EFFECTS: CSS :hover transitions on nodes (slight scale up, shadow increase, border color change). No JavaScript required.

5. TRANSITION SUPPORT: When diagram content changes, animate node position transitions (CSS transition on transform). Requires stable node IDs across renders.

All animations use CSS only (no SMIL, no JavaScript). Respect prefers-reduced-motion. Disabled by default, enabled via config flag.

## Acceptance Criteria

 {"id":"bd-3gq.5","title":"Implement responsive SVG sizing and adaptive detail levels","description":"Make SVG diagrams look great at any size:

1. VIEWBOX-BASED SCALING: SVG uses viewBox with calculated bounds. Width/height set to 100% by default. Container determines rendered size. Preserve aspect ratio.

2. ADAPTIVE DETAIL: At small sizes (diagram area < threshold), automatically simplify: hide edge labels, truncate long node labels, reduce cluster label size. At tiny sizes: show only node shapes without text.

3. FONT SIZE FLOORS: Minimum readable font size (configurable, default 8px). Below this, switch to abbreviated labels or icons-only.

4. BREAKPOINT SYSTEM: Three detail tiers (Compact/Normal/Rich, matching FrankenTUI terminology). Auto-select based on available area. Manual override via config.

5. PRINT OPTIMIZATION: Special print media query styles embedded in SVG. Higher contrast, no animations, crisp borders, black text for readability.

## Acceptance Criteria

- All functionality explicitly described above is implemented fully; no scope reduction, no feature deletions, and no silent behavioral regressions.
- Code is modular, documented where needed, and integrated with existing crates/interfaces without compatibility shims or duplicated logic.

 {"id":"bd-3l8c","title":"Locality-Sensitive Hashing for Approximate Nearest-Node Queries","description":"## Locality-Sensitive Hashing for Approximate Nearest-Node Queries

Implement locality-sensitive hashing (LSH) for fast approximate nearest-node queries in the rendered diagram, enabling O(1) expected-time cursor-to-node proximity detection for interactive editing and hover tooltips.

## Mathematical Foundation
LSH maps similar items (nearby 2D points) to the same hash bucket with high probability. For 2D Euclidean distance, the standard LSH family is:
  h(p) = floor((a · p + b) / w)
where a is a random unit vector, b ~ Uniform[0, w], and w is the bucket width.

**Multi-probe LSH:** Use k hash functions, concatenate their values to form a composite hash. Two points collide (land in same bucket) iff ALL k hash functions agree. Probability of collision for distance d:
  P(collision | distance=d) = (1 - d/w)^k  for d < w, 0 otherwise

**For diagram hit testing:**
Given cursor position (x, y), find the nearest node within radius r. Instead of O(n) linear scan:
1. Hash the cursor position
2. Look up the bucket → candidate nodes
3. Linear scan candidates only (typically O(1) for well-separated nodes)

**Alternative: grid-based spatial hash.** Simpler than LSH but less theoretically elegant:
- Divide viewport into w×w cells
- Each cell stores a list of nodes whose centers fall in it
- Cursor query: check the cell containing cursor + 8 neighbors
- O(1) expected time, but O(n) worst case if all nodes in one cell

**Practical consideration:** For most diagrams (< 1000 nodes), linear scan is fast enough (<1ms). LSH is only justified for 5000+ node diagrams with real-time interaction. Grid spatial hash may be the pragmatic choice.

## Key Papers
- Indyk & Motwani, "Approximate Nearest Neighbors: Towards Removing the Curse of Dimensionality" (STOC 1998)
- Andoni & Indyk, "Near-Optimal Hashing Algorithms for Approximate Nearest Neighbor" (FOCS 2006)
- Datar et al., "Locality-Sensitive Hashing Scheme Based on p-Stable Distributions" (SCG 2004) — E2LSH

## Implementation in FrankenMermaid
1. Implement simple grid spatial hash first (pragmatic baseline).
2. Implement LSH for comparison (educational value + large graph support).
3. Interface: `SpatialIndex::nearest(point, radius) -> Option<NodeId>`
4. Build spatial index after layout, invalidate on edit.
5. Use for: hover detection, click targeting, nearest-node snap in editing mode.
6. Benchmark: query time for cursor movement over 100, 1000, 10000 node diagrams.
7. Decision contract: grid hash for < 5000 nodes, LSH for >= 5000 nodes (or grid hash always if fast enough).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Grid spatial hash implementation with O(1) expected query time
- [ ] LSH implementation with configurable hash parameters
- [ ] Query correctness verified: nearest node matches brute-force result
- [ ] Query time < 0.1ms for 10000-node diagrams
- [ ] Spatial index construction time < 10ms for 10000 nodes
- [ ] Decision contract: grid hash vs LSH with measured thresholds
- [ ] WASM-compatible (no platform-specific dependencies)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:47:12.237351482Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T17:30:09.546873992Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-cs","interaction","lsh","spatial-query"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3l8c","depends_on_id":"bd-17e4.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:30:09.546788211Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3l8c","depends_on_id":"bd-3bc.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:22:52.251507840Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
